design_principle: Design shouldn't be an afterthought, it's a principle.
usability_rule: User expectations are a rule, not a suggestion.
based_on_ubuntu: Based on the latest Ubuntu {{ubuntu_version}} LTS for robust stability and support.
experimental_only: This project is in an experimental status due to multiple technical and legal issues that need to be resolved before a stable release can be made. Do not use this OS if you are not a developer or tester.
support_us: Please consider [buying me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/codeswallop) and [contributing to the project](https://github.com/Proman4713/Utile-OS).
made_with_passion: Made with passion by Aaser Abd-el Sabour (Proman4713).
description_preamble: The Linux desktop scene has been considered unpolished in the eyes of the average macOS or Windows user for *ages*. Trivial things to such users are simply unavailable to them on Linux, at least not by default. Utile is all about *minimal* friction.
description_reasoning: Some of this is caused by accumulated power-user assumptions or 'optimal' design decisions that only fit experienced users, who represent just sub-communities within the *5%* of desktop-using humanity that is the Linux desktop community. Suffice to say that their preferences don't represent those of the 95% on other systems.
description_conclusion: This project aims to reduce friction and increase the intuitiveness of the Linux desktop experience. It is my attempt at creating a more user-friendly Linux desktop experience (as much as I can).
download_description:
The latest major version of Utile OS, built on Ubuntu {{ubuntu_version}} LTS for amazing stability and usability. This release brings the biggest set of improvements and modifications that will shape Utile OS moving forward.

Major Utile releases are feature supported for 1 year, where the system can continue to get Utile-specific updates, bug fixes and improvements. After that, a new major release will be available, which will have a newer Ubuntu LTS base. The current major release will then continue to get updates coming from Ubuntu for the next 4 years, but without any Utile-specific updates.

Of course, this is still an experimental project, so there are no guarantees that this will be the actual support timeline, but this is the plan for now.
reordered_layout: Re-ordered desktop UI layout for familiarity to first-time users
gnome_extensions: Pre-installed and configured GNOME extensions for blur, clipboards, UI features and more...
optimised_keybinds: Optimised keybinds for Windows and macOS migrants
